name: Beef Tenderloin with Spring Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 2 cups beef stock, or canned broth
1 cup chicken stock, or canned low-salt
1 red bell pepper, cut into 8 strips
6 tablespoons chilled unsalted butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
For Beef:
1. Combine beef stock and chicken stock in a medium saucepan. Boil until reduced to 3/4 cup, about 20 minutes. Set aside.
2. Cook carrots in a large saucepan of boiling salted water 3 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer carrots to a strainer and rinse under cold water. Add beans to boiling water; cook 1 minute. Transfer beans to the strainer with carrots and rinse under cold water. Drain well.
3. Arrange carrots, beans, bell pepper, mushrooms, asparagus, and zucchini in a single layer on a large baking sheet. (Stock and vegetables can be prepared 4 hours ahead. Cover stock and vegetables separately and refrigerate.)
4. Preheat the broiler. Sprinkle steaks with salt and pepper. Heat 2 tablespoons olive oil in a heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add steaks; cook to desired doneness, about 4 minutes per side for medium-rare.
5. Transfer steaks to a plate; tent with aluminum foil to keep warm. Add dry red wine to the same skillet. Boil until reduced to 1 tablespoon, scraping up browned bits, about 3 minutes.
6. Add the reduced stock and bring to boil. Remove from heat. Add chilled butter a few pieces at a time and whisk just until melted. Season sauce to taste with salt and pepper.
7. Meanwhile, brush vegetables lightly with olive oil. Broil vegetables until crisp-tender, about 3 minutes per side for carrots, green beans, bell peppers, and mushrooms, and about 2 minutes per side for asparagus and zucchini.
8. Spoon the sauce over vegetables and serve on plates. Place steaks atop the sauce and arrange vegetables decoratively around it.
